title: luzula mannii subsp. mannii
intro: Luzula mannii subsp. mannii, also known as Mann's Woodrush, is a flowering plant species in the family Juncaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and rhizomes have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote overall well-being. Additionally, Luzula mannii subsp. mannii is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
